title: Kate Hudson Returns to Oscars Race with Song Sung Blue
text: Kate Hudson is experiencing a career resurgence 25 years after her breakthrough role in Almost Famous. Her performance in the Neil Diamond tribute film Song Sung Blue has earned her nominations for best actress in a musical or comedy at the Golden Globes and a lead-actress nod from the Actor Awards. Song Sung Blue tells the true story of Mike and Claire Sardina, amateur musicians who meet at the Wisconsin State Fair and form the tribute duo Lightning and Thunder. The film takes a dramatic turn when Claire suffers severe injuries from a car accident, showcasing Hudson's range beyond her typical rom-com roles. The Focus Features release has become a theatrical success story, grossing over 30 million dollars worldwide with a 97% audience score on Rotten Tomatoes. At 46, Hudson views this opportunity differently than her earlier career peak, noting you are always only one part away from the experience reinvigorating itself. She describes her awards campaigning as personal fulfillment, stating it feels like me time dedicated to her craft. Hudson previously shifted away from acting due to limited diverse role opportunities.
